title: ophioglossum vulgatum var. pedunculosum
intro:
Ophioglossum vulgatum var. pedunculosum, also known as the pedunculated adder's tongue fern, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the adder's tongue fern plant. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum var. pedunculosum include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its ability to boost the immune system. 

Herbal preparations of Ophioglossum vulgatum var. pedunculosum typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and rhizomes, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or other medicinal preparations.
